prose: Fukui reactivity analysis of acrolein (8 atoms: O, C, C, C, H, H, H, H) computed at the DFT level using B3LYP/def2-TZVP (PySCF RKS), tier 'standard', gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry was user-supplied (fixed, not re-optimized). Partial charges by Mulliken (PySCF). Density fitting was OFF (exact four-center integrals). N-1 (cation): charge +1, multiplicity 2; N+1 (anion): charge -1, multiplicity 2. All three SCFs converged. Integrity status: ok, trustworthy: true. Warnings array: empty (no warnings). f+ and f- each sum to exactly 1.0000. Headline value is f+ on the most electrophilic atom: C4 (the β-carbon/carbonyl carbon), f+ = 0.64232102. The most nucleophilic site is O1, f- = 0.39388616. The dual descriptor on C4 is 0.5549512, confirming it as the dominant electrophilic center — consistent with acrolein's role as a Michael acceptor. Exact CLI: cli.py fukui [input.xyz] --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1
